<parameter=code>
# Check what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation['nodes']

print("BEFORE white regions:")
for n in before:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  px={n['pixels']}, r={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, c={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

print("\nAFTER white regions:")
for n in after:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  px={n['pixels']}, r={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, c={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

print("\nAFTER colored markers:")
for n in after:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'N', 'p', 'Y'] and n['pixels'] < 10:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, px={n['pixels']}, r={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, c={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Interesting! After clicking blue at cols 28-29:
- White at cols 16-25 (rows 54-57) disappeared
- White at cols 30-33 (rows 56-57) disappeared
- White at cols 28-35 (rows 54-57) appeared (this was the original state!)
- White at cols 18-23 (rows 56-57) appeared (this was the original state!)
So clicking blue at cols 28-29 reversed the effect of clicking blue at cols 24-25! The white regions went back to their original configuration.
Let me summarize what I've learned so far:
1. Blue at cols 12-13: moves track 1 UP (white at cols 6-13 expands up)
2. Blue at cols 16-17: moves track 1 DOWN (white at cols 6-13 shrinks down)
3. Blue at cols 24-25: merges white regions cols 18-23 and 28-35 into cols 16-25 and cols 30-33
4. Blue at cols 28-29: reverses the effect of blue at cols 24-25
Wait, but clicking blue at cols 16-17 also moved track 1 down, which is the opposite of clicking blue at cols 12-13. So maybe the blue blocks come in pairs: one moves up, one moves down.

- White at cols 28-35: controlled by blue at cols 24-25 (left edge) and 28-29 (right edge)
- White at cols 38-47: controlled by blue at cols 28-29 (left edge) and 34-35 (right edge)
- White at cols 50-57: controlled by blue at cols 34-35 (left edge) and 38-39 (right edge)
- White at cols 50-57: also controlled by blue at cols 46-47 and 50-51
Hmm, this is getting confusing. Let me try a different approach. Let me look at the initial state of level 3 and see if I can figure out the goal by looking at the symmetry or pattern.
Actually, I think I need to step back and think about what the game is asking me to do. In levels 1 and 2, the goal was to move a charcoal object to align with a green gap. In level 3, maybe the goal is to move all the colored markers to align with something.
Looking at the colored markers:

- Track 5 (cols 36-37): purple at 39
- Track 2 (cols 14-15): green at 41
- Track 7 (cols 48-49): yellow at 47
- Track 6 (cols 41-43): charcoal at 49-50, purple at 51
- Track 1 (cols 8-10): charcoal at 51-52, green at 53
The markers are at different heights. Maybe the goal is to move them all to the same height. But what height?
Looking at the black bars:

- Black at cols 36-37: gap at row 39 (purple marker is here)

The black bars have gaps at different heights. Maybe the goal is to move the colored markers to fill the gaps in the black bars.
